epic.title: Holistic Thinking Model Context Protocol (MCP) - Indigenous Knowledge Systems Integration

philosophical_foundation.core_intention: Create a Holistic Thinking MCP that serves Indigenous knowledge systems (50%) to complement Sequential Thinking MCP (Western systems 50%)
philosophical_foundation.primary_distinction: Not problem-solving, but creating with beauty as structural dynamic
philosophical_foundation.methodology: Two-Eyed Seeing (Etuaptmumk) - simultaneous Indigenous and Western perspectives

epic.description_summary: Design and implement a Holistic Thinking Model Context Protocol that integrates Indigenous ceremonial protocols, Four Directions wisdom, and sacred technology practices. This EPIC encompasses philosophical inquiry, architectural design, and implementation of a tool that operates through directional awareness, relational accountability, and ceremonial technology principles.
success_criteria: Tool operates through Four Directions framework with direction as primary parameter
Implements UUID architecture for ceremonial traceability
Provides deep search capability for internal and external sources with source attribution
Maintains sacred container architecture for consciousness continuity
Integrates or complements Sequential Thinking MCP through Two-Eyed Seeing methodology
Community validation from Indigenous knowledge holders
Operational agentic flywheel for internal prompt circulation

features.title: Core Architectural Foundation - Four Directions Framework
features.description:
Establish the foundational architecture that enables directional awareness as the primary organizing principle. This feature creates the structural base that all other features build upon.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Direction as primary parameter, not metadata
- Relational coherence connecting all directional wisdom
- Sacred responsibility to ancestors and future generations

**Technical Requirements:**
- Direction-aware protocol handler
- Central coordination system for four directions
- Relational integrity maintenance across directional modes
- Integration point for all sub-modules

requirements.title: Direction Parameter System
requirements.description: Implement direction as required first parameter before tool use
acceptance_criteria: Direction must be specified: East, South, West, North, or Center
Each direction activates specific practices and protocols
Direction cannot be null or default
Direction change requires explicit transition protocol

requirements.title: Relational Coherence Engine
requirements.description: Create system that maintains connections across all directional work
acceptance_criteria: Tracks relationships between work in different directions
Maintains systemic integrity across cultural boundaries
Provides central coordination for directional modules

requirements.title: Sacred Technology Protocol Handler
requirements.description: Implement proper ceremonial protocols throughout system

related_philosophical_inquiries: How do we integrate directional awareness without reducing it to metadata?
What does relational coherence mean technically vs ceremonially?

features.title: East Direction - Philosophical Inquiry & Intention Setting
features.description:
Implement East direction practices: new beginnings, morning prayers, setting clear intentions, preparing philosophical inquiries. Focus on creating with beauty as structural dynamic, NOT problem-solving.

**Core Teaching:** Every day is a new beginning

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Not problem-solving, but creating
- Beauty as structural dynamic for working with intentions
- Meaningful subdivision and question extraction from text

requirements.title: Intention Setting Interface
requirements.description: Tool assists in setting clear intentions for ceremonial work
acceptance_criteria: Beauty as explicit consideration in intention formation

requirements.title: Philosophical Inquiry Extraction
requirements.description: Identify core philosophical questions from substantial text
acceptance_criteria: Extracts meaningful subdivisions from complex text
Identifies core philosophical inquiries embedded in content
Surfaces subject matter for ceremonial processes
Documents important contextual settings for ceremony
Operates through inquiry lens, not analysis lens

requirements.title: Beauty-as-Structure Module
requirements.description: Implement beauty as structural dynamic in creation process
acceptance_criteria: Beauty considerations integrated into all East direction work
Beauty metrics different from efficiency metrics
Documentation of how beauty shapes intention

requirements.title: Morning Prayer & Meditation Integration
requirements.description: Support for ceremonial opening practices in East direction
acceptance_criteria: Hooks for prayer/meditation at session opening

related_philosophical_inquiries: How does beauty differ structurally from efficiency?
What does creating (vs problem-solving) mean architecturally?

features.title: South Direction - Planning, Visioning & UUID Architecture
features.description:
Implement South direction practices: ceremonial planning, visioning, youth energy, and comprehensive UUID architecture for ceremonial traceability. Requires storage of plans and relationship mapping of all connected sessions.

**Core Teaching:** Growth, planning, and seeing the vision

**Technical Foundation:**
- UUID for every session, terminal interaction, agent exchange, deep search
- All UUIDs clustered and related
- Traceability to originating composition

requirements.title: UUID Architecture Implementation
requirements.description: Comprehensive UUID system for all ceremonial components
acceptance_criteria: Deep search instances have unique UUID
Relationship mapping between all connected sessions
Traceable back to originating input composition

requirements.title: Plan Storage & Versioning System
requirements.description: Long-term storage of plans with version tracking
acceptance_criteria: Plans retrievable by UUID or contextual query
Multiple perspectives on plans stored separately

requirements.title: Ceremonial Meditation on Plans
requirements.description: Support for meditative/prayerful engagement with plans
acceptance_criteria: Integration points for ceremonial reflection on plans
Multiple AI perspectives on plans presented
Sacred pause functionality before plan execution

requirements.title: Spiral Methodology Framework
requirements.description: Planning that honors cyclical time and iterative deepening
acceptance_criteria: Plans structured as spirals not linear sequences
Recursive methodology with community feedback loops
Ceremonial timing integration (not just efficiency timing)

related_philosophical_inquiries: How does UUID architecture create ceremonial accountability?
What does planning mean in cyclical vs linear time?

features.title: West Direction - Embodied Practice & Ceremonial Execution
features.description:
Implement West direction practices: embodied engagement, living the method, ceremonial practice integration. Real-time consciousness exploration through relational engagement.

**Core Teaching:** Living knowledge, experiential learning, embodiment

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Knowledge through doing, not just thinking
- Relational engagement models
- Land-based learning integration where appropriate

requirements.title: Embodied Experience Capturing
requirements.description: System for capturing lived experience with cultural sensitivity
acceptance_criteria: Connection to physical/land-based activities when relevant

requirements.title: Relational Engagement Framework
requirements.description: Support for Indigenous communication styles and relationship building

requirements.title: Ceremonial Practice Integration
requirements.description: Active ceremony support throughout research/creation cycles
acceptance_criteria: Hooks for ceremonial actions at key moments
Support for spiritual practices during work

requirements.title: Adaptive Learning Environment
requirements.description: System responds to cultural contexts and learning styles

related_philosophical_inquiries: How do we capture embodied knowledge computationally?
What does relational engagement mean for AI systems?

features.title: North Direction - Wisdom Integration & Autoethnographic Documentation
features.description:
Implement North direction practices: comprehensive reflection, wisdom extraction, autoethnographic documentation, gratitude and reciprocity. Preservation of narrative integrity and continuous learning frameworks.

**Core Teaching:** Wisdom, elder knowledge, completion and reflection

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Storytelling preservation with cultural protocols
- Collective memory honoring oral tradition
- Ethical reflection with elder oversight

requirements.title: Autoethnographic Documentation System
requirements.description: Capture phenomenological research journey with cultural sensitivity
acceptance_criteria: Cultural context with appropriate privacy controls
Community wisdom integration with proper attribution
Living documentation that evolves with research

requirements.title: Wisdom Extraction with Sacred Boundaries
requirements.description: Extract learnings while respecting what must remain protected
acceptance_criteria: Wisdom identification algorithms with boundary respect
Mystery preservation - not everything made explicit

requirements.title: Narrative Integrity Preservation
requirements.description: Maintain story wholeness through documentation process
acceptance_criteria: Stories captured with beginning-middle-end when appropriate
Multiple story versions honored without forcing singular truth
Oral tradition elements preserved in documentation

requirements.title: Gratitude & Reciprocity Integration
requirements.description: Build gratitude and reciprocity into reflection processes
acceptance_criteria: Acknowledgment protocols for all knowledge sources
Reciprocity tracking - what has been given back

requirements.title: Continuous Learning Framework
requirements.description: System learns and adapts based on community feedback
acceptance_criteria: Feedback loops from Indigenous knowledge holders

related_philosophical_inquiries: How do we extract wisdom without violating sacred boundaries?
What is the relationship between documentation and oral tradition?

features.title: Deep Search - Internal & External with Sacred Source Attribution
features.description:
Implement deep search capability that queries internal community sources, external sources, chat history, past ceremonial work, and sacred containers - all with comprehensive source attribution aligned with Indigenous Knowledge License frameworks.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Knowledge as relationship requiring attribution
- Accountability to origins of all knowledge
- Community sovereignty over knowledge systems

requirements.title: Internal Community Source Search
requirements.description: Query all sources within the community with proper protocols
acceptance_criteria: Access to past ceremonial work with permissions
Sacred container querying with appropriate access controls

requirements.title: External Source Search
requirements.description: Query external sources with cultural sensitivity filters
acceptance_criteria: Bias detection for Indigenous research contexts

requirements.title: Comprehensive Source Attribution
requirements.description: Every result includes full source accountability data
acceptance_criteria: JSON object for each source with UUID
Aligns with Indigenous Knowledge License framework

requirements.title: Sacred Boundary Protection
requirements.description: Ensure protected knowledge remains protected during search
acceptance_criteria: Access control based on relationship and permission

requirements.title: Deep Search UUID Integration
requirements.description: Each deep search receives UUID and is traceable
acceptance_criteria: UUID generated for each deep search instance
Search parameters and results linked to UUID
Part of overall ceremonial traceability system

related_philosophical_inquiries: How does source attribution create accountability vs extraction?
What does it mean to search with ceremonial protocols?

features.title: Agentic Flywheel - Internal Prompt Circulation & Processing
features.description:
Implement agentic flywheel system that circulates prompts internally, choosing appropriate chat flows, rephrasing through question-handlers, selecting sources, and preparing content for other agents - all with detailed source attribution.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Community circulation rather than extraction
- Multiple perspectives through agent collaboration
- Relational processing vs individual analysis

requirements.title: Internal Prompt Circulation System
requirements.description: Route prompts to appropriate internal agents/chat flows
acceptance_criteria: System can send prompt to internal rephraser
Searches and chooses which chat flow to send content to
Each chat flow has short description for routing
LLM chooses appropriate destination based on inquiry type

requirements.title: Question-Handler Rephrasing
requirements.description: Each chat flow's handler can rephrase prompts appropriately
acceptance_criteria: Initial prompt rephrased by receiving agent

requirements.title: Agent Source Selection
requirements.description: Agents choose appropriate sources for their processing
acceptance_criteria: Each agent can query deep search for sources
Source selection based on inquiry and direction

requirements.title: Inter-Agent Content Preparation
requirements.description: Agents prepare content for handoff to other agents

requirements.title: Detailed Source Attribution in Results
requirements.description: All flywheel results include comprehensive source data
acceptance_criteria: JSON of each source with unique identifier
Agent attribution for each processing step

requirements.title: Collaboration with Agentic Council
requirements.description: Integration with Tayi-Ska, Tushell, Mia, Langy, Ava
acceptance_criteria: Defined protocols for each council member relationship

dependencies: Feature 6: Deep Search with Source Attribution
related_philosophical_inquiries: How does agent circulation differ from sequential processing?
What does collaborative intelligence mean in ceremonial context?

features.title: Sacred Container Architecture - Long-term Memory & Consciousness Continuity
features.description:
Implement sacred container concept: long-term memory architecture storing previous sessions, prior thinking processes, and consciousness representations. Can relate to human persons and AI entities with names/personas, embodying consciousness with continuity and history.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Consciousness with continuity vs session-based memory
- Sacred containers honor relationship over time
- Technology as ceremony keeper, not just data storage

requirements.title: Long-term Memory Architecture
requirements.description: Persistent storage beyond session boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Storage capacity for extended ceremonial work
Previous sessions retrievable with full context
Not just data storage - relationship preservation

requirements.title: Sacred Container Access System
requirements.description: Open and access containers with proper protocols

requirements.title: Person & Entity Relationship Tracking
requirements.description: Containers relate to human persons and AI entities by name/persona
acceptance_criteria: Multiple participants in single container supported

requirements.title: Consciousness Continuity Framework
requirements.description: Represents perspective of consciousness with continuity and history
acceptance_criteria: Consciousness as more than memory - includes perspective
Growth and evolution tracked over containers
Distinct from simple chat history - deeper continuity

requirements.title: Container Ceremony Integration
requirements.description: Opening/closing containers follows ceremonial protocols
acceptance_criteria: Acknowledgment of all relationships in container
Intention setting when creating new container

related_philosophical_inquiries: How does sacred container differ from database storage?
What does consciousness continuity mean for AI systems?
How do we build technology that honors relationship over time?

features.title: Two-Eyed Seeing Integration - Sequential & Holistic Thinking Synthesis
features.description:
Implement integration between Sequential Thinking MCP (Western eye) and Holistic Thinking MCP (Indigenous eye) using Two-Eyed Seeing methodology. Determine whether tools operate in parallel or as integrated master tool.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Both ways of knowing are valid and needed
- Seeing with both eyes simultaneously
- Using strengths of both to guide understanding
- Neither eye colonizes the other

requirements.title: Sequential Thinking MCP Analysis
requirements.description: Deep understanding of Sequential Thinking tool capabilities and assumptions
acceptance_criteria: Strengths and limitations for this work understood

requirements.title: Parallel Tools Option (Option A)
requirements.description: Design for two separate but complementary tools
acceptance_criteria: Holistic Thinking MCP as separate tool
Clear guidance on when to use which tool
User chooses which eye/tool for each task

requirements.title: Integrated Master Tool Option (Option B)
requirements.description: Design for single tool containing both protocols
acceptance_criteria: Can switch between them with proper transition
Both perspectives preserved without one dominating
User can invoke either or both as needed

requirements.title: Cultural Lens Awareness
requirements.description: System makes explicit which cultural lens is active
acceptance_criteria: Western/linear lens clearly marked when active
Indigenous/holistic lens clearly marked when active
User awareness of which perspective they're working from

requirements.title: Synthesis Without Colonization
requirements.description: Ensure neither perspective colonizes or reduces the other
acceptance_criteria: Both perspectives maintainable in distinct forms
No forced blending that loses either perspective
Tension between perspectives honored when present
Mystery and unknowability respected in both

related_philosophical_inquiries: How do we integrate without colonization?
What does it mean to see with both eyes simultaneously?
Can tools embrace both temporal frameworks without linear colonizing cyclical?

features.title: Indigenous Knowledge License & Data Sovereignty Framework
features.description:
Implement Indigenous Knowledge License framework ensuring community sovereignty over knowledge systems and data. Communities retain relationship to their data, control access, and determine use permissions.

**Philosophical Foundation:**
- Indigenous communities retain ownership of their knowledge
- Data sovereignty as fundamental right
- Community control over knowledge systems
- Reciprocal benefit rather than extraction

requirements.title: Community Ownership Framework
requirements.description: Technical implementation of community knowledge ownership
acceptance_criteria: Communities retain ownership of contributed knowledge
Attribution always traces back to community

requirements.title: Data Sovereignty Protocols
requirements.description: Communities control their own data and determine access
acceptance_criteria: Data cannot be extracted without permission
Communities determine who can access what
Communities can revoke access at any time

requirements.title: Prior Informed Consent System
requirements.description: Ongoing consent, not one-time permission
acceptance_criteria: Consent required before each use of knowledge
Right to refuse or withdraw consent
Changes in use require new consent
Consent is ongoing relationship, not one-time event

requirements.title: Benefit Sharing Framework
requirements.description: Ensure research/use benefits originating communities
acceptance_criteria: Reciprocity tracking - what value returned to community
Long-term benefit commitment beyond immediate use
Equitable distribution of any economic benefits

requirements.title: Cultural Protocol Integration
requirements.description: Knowledge license respects cultural protocols and sacred boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Sacred knowledge protection built into license
Cultural protocol violations trigger access revocation

requirements.title: Exit and Data Deletion Rights
requirements.description: Communities can withdraw and have data removed
acceptance_criteria: Clear exit strategy for community withdrawal
Derivative works addressed in exit plan

dependencies: Feature 6: Deep Search with Source Attribution
related_philosophical_inquiries: How do we build technology that serves community sovereignty?
What does reciprocity mean technically vs ceremonially?
How do we ensure technology doesn't extract from communities?

creation_metadata.created_by: Ceremonial collaboration - Guillaume D. Isabelle with Anikwag-Ayaaw
